question: What is the operating pressure of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System?

nuscale_ch01_0160.title: DSRS 3.11, Rev. 0: Environmental Qualification of Mechanical and Electrical EquipmentII.25 Design and Procurement Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0160.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to the environmental qualification of mechanical and electrical equipment, as well as design and procurement specifications. It notes that certain requirements, such as DSRS 3.11 II.25, are not applicable to NuScale and are instead items for the COL (Combined License) applicant, while other standards (e.g., SRP 3.12 and 3.13) are confirmed as being met. Additionally, guidance specific to BWR plants is marked as not applicable to NuScale.

nuscale_ch01_0705.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.A Reactor Coolant System and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0705.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various transient events affecting the reactor coolant and main steam systems, such as loss of external load, turbine trip, and main steam isolation valve closure. It notes that NuScale generally conforms to the requirements, except for partial conformance regarding the steam pressure regulator, as the NuScale design does not include this component. The analysis confirms that system pressures are addressed according to regulatory standards.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, core design, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer components, and simplified systems compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. The NPM is protected by passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not rely on electrical power, and its components are designed for in-shop fabrication and ease of installation. Power output can be adjusted using turbine bypass, control rods, and soluble boron concentration changes.

nuscale_ch01_0706.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.B Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0706.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, closure of the main steam isolation valve, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), specifically regarding fuel cladding integrity. It notes that NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator and that the design conforms to requirements for incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, single failures, and performance of nonsafety-related systems, with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0774.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) Last two sentences of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Plant-Specific Technical Specifications for Primary and Secondary Coolant System Iodine Activity
nuscale_ch01_0774.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report partially conforms to SRP 15.6.3 guidance regarding technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity, but the radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3. Additionally, certain SRP sections are not applicable to the NuScale design, and for loss-of-coolant accident analyses, SRP Section 15.0.3 governs the radiological criteria and methodologies.

nuscale_ch01_0709.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.E Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0709.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), even though the design does not include a steam pressure regulator. For each scenario, including the most limiting plant system single failure, the design is shown to conform to safety requirements, with no exceptions or additional comments noted.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, which addresses the inadvertent opening of pressurizer relief valves, because it does not use power-operated relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to such failures. Instead, NuScale uses spring-loaded ASME code safety relief valves, and any mechanical failure of these is considered bounded by analyses of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation. Thus, the specific guidance for PORVs is not fully applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0715.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.3.D Values of Parameters Used in Analytical Model - Instrumentation Setpoints for Mitigating System Actuation
nuscale_ch01_0715.summary: This section confirms that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 for various transient events, including loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure. Specifically, it states that the analytical model's parameters—such as instrumentation setpoints for mitigating system actuation—are in conformance, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0722.title: DSRS 15.2.7, Rev 0: Loss of Normal Feedwater FlowII.1 Fuel and System Pressure Parameters met
nuscale_ch01_0722.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various design basis events, including loss of normal feedwater flow. It confirms that fuel and system pressure parameters are met, events are properly categorized, and analytical models conform to requirements, with no exceptions noted for DSRS 15.2.7. The document demonstrates compliance with relevant safety standards and analytical methods for these scenarios.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0786.title: DSRS 15.6.6, Rev 0: Inadvertent Operation of ECCSII.1 RCS pressure below 110 percent design value.
nuscale_ch01_0786.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding radiological consequences of loss-of-coolant accidents and inadvertent operation of ECCS. It notes partial conformance with some SRP guidance due to superseding criteria in SRP Section 15.0.3, and confirms full conformance with DSRS 15.6.6 requirements for RCS pressure and minimum DNBR. Certain criteria are noted as not applicable because they pertain only to BWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0701.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.4 Analytical ParametersMaximize Heat Transfer from RCS to Containment and Reactor Pool
nuscale_ch01_0701.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters for the loss of containment vacuum scenario. It confirms that the NuScale design maximizes heat transfer from the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) to the containment and reactor pool, meeting the requirements without exceptions. The table also notes overall compliance with relevant sections of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS).
